sources.name: NASA
sources.license_summary: Public domain — works of the United States Government (17 U.S.C. § 105).

sources.name: European Space Agency
sources.license_summary: Creative Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO for most imagery; some material under CC BY 4.0 IGO. Logos under permissive ESA emblem terms.

sources.name: Roscosmos
sources.license_summary: Imagery and Soviet-era space photographs are in the public domain in Russia (Article 1259, Russian Civil Code).

sources.name: China National Space Administration
sources.license_summary: Imagery commonly redistributed via Creative Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 on Wikimedia Commons; PRC government works on the official site are released for editorial reuse.

sources.name: Indian Space Research Organisation
sources.license_summary: Government of India works (GODL-India) commonly redistributed under permissive terms; ISRO mission imagery is freely usable for editorial purposes with credit.

sources.name: Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency
sources.license_summary: Creative Commons CC BY 4.0 for most JAXA digital archive imagery; some assets under JAXA's editorial-reuse policy.

sources.name: SpaceX
sources.license_summary: SpaceX official launch and mission photos historically released as public domain (CC0); company logos under PD-trivial.

sources.name: Blue Origin
sources.license_summary: Mixed: official Blue Origin imagery used under CC BY-SA via Wikimedia Commons; partnership photographs taken by NASA staff are public domain (works of the U.S. Government).

sources.name: MBRSC / UAE Space Agency
sources.license_summary: Creative Commons CC BY 4.0 for Mohammed bin Rashid Space Centre imagery (Hope Mars Mission, Rashid lunar rover).

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons contributors
sources.license_summary: Mixed: public domain, CC0, CC BY 2.0/2.5/3.0/4.0, CC BY-SA 2.0/2.5/3.0/4.0, CC BY 3.0/4.0 IGO, CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO. Each image entry below carries its specific license.

sources.name: Wikipedia contributors
sources.license_summary: Creative Commons CC BY-SA 4.0. Editorial copy paraphrased from Wikipedia carries attribution and a link to the original article.

sources.name: Solar System Scope
sources.license_summary: Planet and small-body texture maps released under Creative Commons CC BY 4.0.

sources.name: Canadian Space Agency
sources.license_summary: Government of Canada works (CSA-NCO non-commercial reuse policy); editorial reuse with credit is permitted.

sources.name: NOAA
sources.license_summary: Public domain — works of the United States Government (17 U.S.C. § 105). NOAA imagery is freely usable with credit.

sources.name: U.S. Space Force
sources.license_summary: Public domain — works of the United States Government. Outbound reference links to gps.gov for GPS constellation operations.

sources.name: Space Telescope Science Institute
sources.license_summary: Hubble (hubblesite.org) and Webb (webbtelescope.org) outreach material released for editorial reuse with credit; STScI operates under NASA contract.

sources.name: Nature (Springer Nature)
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no Nature copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: Science (AAAS)
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no AAAS copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: Elsevier ScienceDirect
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no Elsevier copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: IOP Publishing
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no IOP copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: Annual Reviews
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no Annual Reviews copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: Springer
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no Springer copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: National Academies Press
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; National Academies reports are public records of consensus committees.

sources.name: IEEE Xplore
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no IEEE copy is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: The Planetary Society
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; The Planetary Society is a non-profit science-advocacy publisher.

sources.name: IPGP / SEIS Insight
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links to the SEIS / Mars InSight science consortium hosted at IPGP.

sources.name: Boeing
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no Boeing copy or imagery is redistributed in-app.

sources.name: United Launch Alliance
sources.license_summary: Outbound reference links only; no ULA copy or imagery is redistributed in-app.
